prs.title: fix: enforce nested VPC hierarchy and drag-and-drop rules
feat: use double-click for opening node properties sidebar
prs.body:
## Description
This PR improves the canvas User Experience by decoupling node selection from the properties sidebar behavior. Previously, single-clicking a node immediately opened the right sidebar, which caused jarring layout shifts during rapid node selection or multi-select workflows.

## Changes Included
* **Single Click Selection:** Clicking a node once now *only* selects the node visually on the canvas.
* **Double Click Properties:** Added the `onNodeDoubleClick` event listener to `<ReactFlow>`. The right-hand properties sidebar will now only slide open when a user intentionally double-clicks a specific node.
* **Selection Clearing:** Maintained the existing behavior where deselecting nodes (clicking the empty canvas background) smoothly closes the sidebar if it is currently open.
* **State Management:** Refactored `WorkspaceLayout.tsx` to handle the new explicit open triggers rather than binding the panel strictly to the `selectedNodeId` lifecycle.

## Testing Instructions
1. Single-click a node. Verify it is selected (blue border) but the right sidebar does *not* open.
2. Double-click the node. Verify the properties sidebar slides open.
3. Click the empty canvas background. Verify the sidebar closes.

prs.title: feat: implement copy-paste and multi-select for nodes
prs.body:
## Description
This PR introduces native copy-paste functionality to the canvas, significantly speeding up topology creation, alongside multi-select capabilities.

## Changes Included
* **`useCopyPaste` Hook:** Added custom logic to intercept `Cmd/Ctrl+C` and `Cmd/Ctrl+V` events.
* **Cursor-Targeted Pasting:** Pasted nodes are intelligently calculated to drop exactly where the user's mouse cursor is pointing, accounting for zoom and pan states via React Flow's `screenToFlowPosition`.
* **Deep ID Mapping:** When nodes are duplicated, they are assigned new unique UUIDs via `crypto.randomUUID()`. A mapping dictionary ensures that:
  * Internal edges between copied nodes are preserved and accurately re-linked to the newly generated nodes.
  * Parent/Child relationships (e.g., nodes inside a VPC) are accurately preserved for the duplicated group.
* **Shift Multi-Select:** Enabled `multiSelectionKeyCode="Shift"` in the `ReactFlow` component, allowing users to lasso or click multiple nodes easily before copying.

## Testing Instructions
1. Hold `Shift` and click multiple nodes to ensure multi-select works.
2. Press `Cmd/Ctrl+C` to copy the selected nodes.
3. Move your cursor to a blank space on the canvas and press `Cmd/Ctrl+V`.
4. Verify the nodes appear precisely under your cursor, with unique IDs, and that any edges between them remain intact.

feat: dynamically fetch repositories from GitHub API
prs.body:
---
title: "[PR]: Dynamic Repository Fetching for Projects Page"
labels: 'enhancement'
assignees: ''
---

## Summary

Enhanced the Projects page to dynamically fetch repositories from the GitHub API instead of using hardcoded data. This ensures the projects page automatically stays up-to-date with all repositories in the XplnHUB organization without requiring manual code updates.

**Key Changes:**
- Implemented dynamic fetching from `https://api.github.com/orgs/XplnHUB/repos`
## Related Issues

Closes #6

## Testing

- [x] `npm run lint` - Passed
- [x] Manual testing:
  - Verified repositories load dynamically from GitHub API
  -  Confirmed loading spinner displays while fetching
  -  Tested error state by simulating network failure
  - 
**Loading State:**
- Displays animated spinner with "Loading projects..." message

**Loaded State:**
- All XplnHUB repositories displayed with live data from GitHub
- Shows repository name, language, description, stars, and forks

issues.title: Mobile text overflowing
issues.body: ## 🐞 Bug Description On smaller screens (mobile view), the **problem description text** inside each topic card is overflowing and getting cut off, making it hard to read. The text alignment and spacing are not responsive. --- ## 📋 Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the website on a mobile device or in Chrome DevTools responsive mode. 2. Set the dimensions to iPhone 12 Pro or any small-width device. 3. Scroll down to the **problem cards** section (e.g., “Maximum Subarray”, “Product of Array Except Self”). 4. Observe that the description text is overflowing and misaligned. --- ## 🎯 Expected Behavior The text should wrap properly inside the card container and stay fully visible without overflowing. Padding and font size should adjust dynamically for smaller screen widths to maintain readability. --- ## 📸 Screenshots *(Refer to attached screenshot for visual reference.)* --- ## 💻 Environment - **OS:** macOS Sonoma 15.0 - **Browser:** Google Chrome 141.0 - **Device Tested:** iPhone 12 Pro (Responsive mode) - **Version:** Live site version as of October 2025 --- ## ✅ Additional Context - The issue likely occurs due to fixed text width or lack of word-wrap inside the card container. - Could be fixed using CSS properties.

issues.title: Add a mobile navbar
issues.body:
## ✨ Feature Description

Improve the **mobile navigation experience** by redesigning the current navigation bar. 

---

## 🎯 Why is this Needed?

- The current mobile navigation feels cramped and inconsistent in layout.  
- Buttons and links are too close together, making touch interaction difficult.  
- The transition between desktop and mobile views isn’t smooth.  
- A better UX will help users explore the site effortlessly, especially from mobile devices.

---

## 📋 Possible Implementation

- Introduce a **hamburger menu** for compact mobile navigation.  
- Add smooth **slide-in / fade animations** for menu open/close actions.  
- Optionally, add **sticky navigation** so users can access it easily while scrolling.  

---

issues.title: Add a clear error message when the GEMINI_API_KEY is missing

Make a section in the feature page that this happens when Gemini API is not setup so that when users read the documentation so they do not miss the imp step

issues.title: Develop an interactive web dashboard for report visualization
issues.body:
An interactive web dashboard would significantly improve the user experience . The dashboard could provide features like :
- a way to upload or view the generated Markdown reports.
- display the summary and detailed file reports in a user-friendly interface.
- interactive elements, such as charts for lines of code, number of functions, etc.

The dashboard could be a separate web application that consumes the output of the insight CLI tool.

issues.title: Add support for exporting analysis reports as HTML and PDF
issues.body: The current version of insight generates reports in Markdown (.md) format. While this is useful, adding the ability to export reports in other formats like HTML and PDF would make them more shareable and accessible to a wider audience. An HTML report could be interactive, and a PDF report would be ideal for printing and formal documentation.

issues.title: Add Responsive Animated Hamburger Menu (Slides in from Right)
issues.body:
Implement a mobile-friendly hamburger menu that :
- Is visible only on small screens
- Animates in/out smoothly
- Slides in from the right side
- Includes a close (X) button
- Disables background scroll when open

issues.title: Incorrect README file
issues.body: Currently the README file doesn't have any information so users can't get a glimpse of what the webpage is about , so create/update the README file

issues.title: Social media and mail icons not linked to any URLs
issues.body: In the Campus Clubs section on the club cards, the social media icons (YouTube, Instagram, and Mail) are displayed but currently do not reference or link to any social media profiles or email addresses. Add proper href attributes to the icons, linking to the correct social media URLs and mailto link. Ensure the icons are clickable and accessible.

issues.title: Update contact information in the FOOTER
issues.body: The footer currently contains incorrect contact information such as the phone number and email address. Please update it with the correct details, and consider adding any additional relevant contact information as needed.

issues.title: Footer Misalignment When Fetching Events
issues.body: When the events are being fetched (during the loading state), the footer appears misaligned or floats above the bottom of the page. This happens because there isn't enough content on the page to push the footer down. **Fix -** The layout should be adjusted so that the footer stays at the bottom of the viewport even when content is loading or minimal. This can be handled with layout styling improvements.

issues.title: Navbar Logo Routing to Homepage
issues.body:
Description:
The logo in the navbar currently does not redirect users to the homepage. Users expect clicking the logo to return them to the main landing page.

**Fix -**
Wrap the navbar logo element in a link that routes to the homepage (/). Ensure that the routing works correctly with the framework being used .

issues.title: Implement Smooth Scroll
issues.body:
Implementing smooth scrolling will improve navigation and enhance the overall user experience.

**Fix -**
Update the frontend to enable smooth scrolling, either by applying CSS properties or integrating a JavaScript smooth-scroll library, ensuring compatibility across major browsers.

issues.title: Split Sections into Separate Pages & Expand Content
issues.body:
The website currently has only 3 sections — Features, Implementation, and Roadmap — all placed on a single landing page.
Users must scroll through everything in one page.
Limited information is provided per section.
Website feels more like a static showcase rather than a detailed project hub.

Problem:
Navigation is not user-friendly for detailed reading.
Visitors don’t get enough context about the project.
Lack of dedicated pages reduces SEO and discoverability.

**Fix -**
Set up proper routing
Move each major section into its own page component.
Update Navbar & Footer to include links to these pages.
Optionally keep a short summary of each section on the landing page that links to the full page.

issues.title: Missing About Page / Section
issues.body:
The website currently has no About page or section, leaving users to interpret the purpose and functionality of the project on their own.
New visitors don’t immediately understand what the project is about.
Lack of context reduces engagement and credibility.
No central place to explain the vision, features, or usage.

**Fix -**

Add a dedicated About page (or section on the homepage) that provides:
A short overview of the project.
The problem it solves and its main features.
Optionally, a “How it works” or workflow explanation.
Links to relevant docs, GitHub repo, or contributors.

issues.title: Improve "Supported File Types" Section for Better UX
issues.body: The section currently shows a large grid of file extensions (.py, .js, .ts, etc.), which is cluttered and harder for new users to understand at a glance. Listing raw file types doesn’t clearly communicate languages supported. Users have to interpret file extensions to languages. The UI looks crowded and doesn’t provide deeper context (e.g., how files are evaluated). **Fix :** Show a concise list like “Supports 20+ Languages” (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Java, etc.). On click, a popup / modal should open that: - Lists all supported file extensions grouped by language. - Optionally explains how evaluation works for each language.

issues.title: Text Alignment and Animation Issues in Activate Virtual env. Tab
issues.body: In the “Environment Activated” step of the GET STARTED, the text and UI elements are not aligned properly. This makes the section look broken and less professional. **Fix -** The “Environment Activated” message should be centered and aligned consistently with the rest of the stepper content. All text and buttons should maintain equal spacing from the edges. The layout should stay clean and balanced on desktop and mobile.

issues.title: Mouse Pointer Lag on Fast Movement
issues.body:
The website currently uses a custom mouse pointer/animation effect.
When the mouse moves slowly, the behavior is smooth.
However, when moving the mouse quickly across the screen, the pointer effect becomes laggy and unresponsive, making the site feel less polished.
**Fix -**
The pointer should follow the mouse instantly and smoothly without delay.
Even during quick movements, it should feel fluid, responsive, and natural.
No stuttering, frame drops, or jitter should be noticeable.

issues.title: Convert Static Site into Multi-Page Layout
issues.body:
Currently, the website is a single-page static site, making it hard to organize content and provide detailed information.
Navigation is limited, and users cannot directly access specific sections via URLs.
Split the site into multiple pages such as Home, Features, About and Contact.
Use a shared layout so the navbar and footer remain consistent across pages.
Implement proper routing so each page has its own URL (e.g., /features, /about).

issues.title: Improve Footer with Detailed Information
issues.body:
The current footer is minimal and lacks essential information like quick links, contact details, and social media links.
Users cannot easily access important resources or get in touch, which reduces usability.
Add sections such as Quick Links, Contact Info, About, and optional Social Media Links.
Ensure the footer is responsive and consistent across all pages.

issues.title: Navbar Logo Should Redirect to Home Page
issues.body:
Currently, the logo in the navbar is static and does not perform any action when clicked.
Users generally expect that clicking the site logo takes them back to the homepage, so the current behavior can be confusing and reduces navigation efficiency.
This applies to all screen sizes, including both desktop and mobile.
Clicking the navbar logo should route the user to the Home Page (/).
Make sure the clickable area covers the entire logo, so users don’t have to only click on the center.

issues.title: Text Misalignment in Feature Cards
issues.body: The text inside the feature cards on the “Why Choose CampusBuddy?” section is not vertically aligned consistently. The spacing between icons, headings, and descriptions varies slightly across the cards. All cards should have uniform alignment and spacing for a better look.

issues.title: Mobile Optimization
issues.body:
The events page when viewed on the mobile is too zoomed and the UI is not optimized for vertical screens .

The filter section protrude out too much , the ongoing events section past event section comes in between the Events and footer is also not optimized for the device width.

issues.title: Presence of Empty & Unnecessary Files in Repo
issues.body:
**Description:**
The repository currently contains empty files and unused/unnecessary files that do not contribute to the project’s functionality.
These files create unwanted bulk, make the repo harder to navigate, and may confuse developers about which files are relevant.
They also increase repo size, which impacts cloning, builds, and maintainability.

Identify and remove all empty files that serve no purpose.
Clean up unused files or empty files(for ex. react.svg present in assets folder).

issues.title: Outdated & Missing Clubs on /clubpage
issues.body:
**Description:**
On the /clubpage, some old/deactivated clubs (e.g., Astro Club) are still being displayed even though they are no longer active.
At the same time, newly created clubs (e.g., AI Club) are not visible in the listing.
This creates confusion for users because they see clubs that don’t exist anymore while missing out on opportunities to discover new and active clubs.

Ensure /clubpage only shows currently active clubs.
Remove deactivated clubs (like Astro Club) from the display.
Add new and recently formed clubs (like AI Club)

issues.title: Mobile Navbar & Hamburger Menu Improvements
issues.body:
**Description:**
In the mobile version, the hamburger menu is not well-positioned.
Options like "My Events" and "Login/Profile" are missing from the hamburger dropdown, rather they are on the right of the hamburger making everything very cramped up.

Fix the location/placement of the hamburger menu so it appears in a more standard and easily accessible spot (top-right corner of the mobile navbar).
Move key navigation items such as “My Events” and “Login/Profile” inside the hamburger menu.
Ensure the hamburger menu expands smoothly and closes properly after selecting an option.

issues.title: Upon Clicking the Top Club Card on the Home Page Does Nothing
issues.body:
Description:

Currently, clicking on a club card does nothing, just shows a mouse pointer.

**Either :**
- Club cards should redirect to the respective Club Page (already available on /clubpage) so that users can explore full details about the club.
- Make a "more info" button either in the club cards or at the bottom which would navigate the user to the **/clubpage** for detailed info.

This will make navigation more intuitive and allow users to learn more about clubs directly from the main listing.

issues.title: Inaccurate Club Information
issues.body:
**Description:**

- The number of members displayed for clubs is not accurate or up to date.
- The club descriptions are too short, vague, and do not properly represent what the club actually does.


Show the real-time, correct member count for each club.
Provide detailed and informative descriptions that give users a clear idea about the club’s purpose, activities, and achievements.
This is especially important for Top Clubs, as their information should feel reliable and well-presented.

issues.title: Update Events State and Add Upcoming Events
issues.body:
Currently, all events are marked as upcoming even though they are already completed in the past.

Tasks:
1. Update the state of past events from upcoming → past.
2. Add new events with correct upcoming status.
3. Ensure event status updates automatically based on the event date.


Past events should show under Past Events.

Upcoming events should be visible in a separate section with correct details.

issues.title: Event Name and Description Showing Gibberish
issues.body: This particular event organised by debate soceity in the Events Page are displaying gibberish text for the name and description. Event name and description should be properly fetched and displayed in readable text. If data is missing or corrupted, show a placeholder (e.g., “No description available”).

issues.title: Fix Infinite Loading on Events Page When No Events Attended
issues.body:
Currently, if a student has not attended any events, the Events Page gets stuck on loading and never resolves.

If no events are found, give a pop up of “No events attended yet” message or an empty state illustration.

Ensure the page does not remain in a loading state indefinitely.

issues.title: Fix CSS Styling for Cancel Button in the profile section
issues.body: The Cancel button in the profile edit popup has inconsistent styling compared to the Save button (different background, size, and alignment). Both buttons should have consistent styles (padding, border-radius, background color). Cancel button can use a neutral color (e.g., red) to differentiate from Save but still match in shape and size.

issues.title: Create Profile Page instead of Popup
issues.body: Currently, the profile opens in a popup. Replace it with a dedicated Profile Page where users can: View and edit their details. Upload/change their profile photo. See events attended, attendance rate, and membership info in a cleaner layout. This will improve usability and personalization.

issues.title: Fix the footer that contains the "SUBSCRIBE TO OUR NEWSLETTER"
issues.body: This is affecting the width of the page thus if the website is used in lesser aspect ratio than a desktop browser then it affects the user experience.

issues.title: NEWSLETTER ISSUE : Error 403: Expired LinkedIn Document URL
issues.body:
Opening the NEWSLETTER the LinkedIn post link are having some issues . 
Accessing a LinkedIn-hosted PDF returns a 403 "deny-expired-url" error, indicating the document link has expired and needs to be regenerated.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ecec767-8889-49c6-8db1-fc4b5d1940c0)

issues.title: 404 Error on /Cubesat/Cubesat.html - Page Not Found on Deployed Site
issues.body:
Accessing the URL https://website-sastcomp.vercel.app/Cubesat/Cubesat.html leads to a 404: NOT_FOUND error.

Error ID: bom1::d1hsv-1748939026458-395e39d6a280

Please verify the issue in the Projects.jsx file in components folder in src.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2557ed93-8057-4c57-a3e4-d75f26e94707)

issues.title: 404 Error on /projects Route - Page Not Found on Deployed Site
issues.body:
When trying to access the /projects route on the deployed site https://website-sastcomp.vercel.app/projects, I get a 404: NOT_FOUND error.

Error ID: bom1::qgb75-1748937751177-5b3cc3acb906
Please check if the /projects route is correctly configured in the Next.js app and if the page exists or is properly routed.
